title: SEC2: IDB stores JSON-in-string instead of native object
description:
File: wasm/src/storage.rs lines 90-92 (save) and lines 123-125 (load).

save_identity serializes the base64 secret into a serde_json::Value, converts it to a JSON string, then stores that string as a JsValue in IndexedDB. On load, the string is retrieved, then serde_json::from_str is called to parse it back.

Cost: one extra serde_json::to_string allocation on every save; one extra JsValue::as_string() + serde_json::from_str parse on every load. Load happens at app startup on every page load — users feel this as initialization latency. The parse also brings serde_json into the startup hot path unnecessarily for a simple 64-byte blob.

Fix: Use js_sys::Object and js_sys::Reflect::set to construct a native JS object {secret_b64: '...'} directly, then store that JsValue. On load, use js_sys::Reflect::get to extract the field without JSON parsing. This removes the JSON roundtrip from the IDB storage path entirely.

Classify: P2 (extra parse on every app startup; also cleaner -- IndexedDB is designed to store structured objects natively)
close_reason: By design: JSON string storage is simpler and more portable. Identity load/save occurs at most once per session; optimization would add complexity for unmeasurable benefit. Defer to Phase 2 if profiling shows actual overhead.
